confidence_rationale: Canonical high-quality review in a premier journal. Directly documents hippocampal-cortical dialogue during NREM SWS as the mechanistic basis of memory consolidation; covers sharp-wave ripple replay, thalamo-cortical sleep spindles, and slow oscillation coordination. Maps cleanly onto MECH-030's core claim that offline consolidation uses hippocampal-cortical replay while suppressing active online processing. Confidence docked slightly because the REE sleep mode is explicitly V4 scope, adding transfer risk.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During NREM slow-wave sleep, hippocampal sharp-wave ripples reactivate recent experience representations and, coupled with thalamo-cortical sleep spindles nested within cortical slow oscillations, drive transfer of labile hippocampal memories to stable neocortical long-term storage. This active systems consolidation is suppressed during online waking states.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-030 requires an offline sleep/consolidation mode in which the system replays recently accumulated trajectory and residue representations via a hippocampal-cortical channel (analog of SWR-driven replay) while commit signals and new action commitments are suppressed. Diekelmann & Born provide the biological substrate for exactly this architecture: (1) replay is internally generated and offline, not driven by new sensory input; (2) the neocortical write phase requires suppression of ongoing processing; (3) consolidation recalibrates which representations survive into long-term storage -- mapping onto MECH-030's precision recalibration and residue contextualisation functions.
mapping.mapping_caveat: MECH-030 and the REE sleep mode are explicitly V4 scope -- not implemented in V1, V2, or V3 substrates. This mapping is architectural/aspirational: the biological evidence motivates the design, but no REE experiment has yet tested any prediction of MECH-030. Additionally, the biological replay timescale (hours of sleep, multi-night consolidation) is much longer than any computational analog likely to be feasible in REE experiments.
mapping.source_context: Review covers rodent and human sleep memory research; NREM SWS consolidation of declarative/episodic memory; REM sleep role in procedural and emotional memory; synaptic tagging and capture framework; clinical relevance of sleep disruption on memory.

confidence_components.notes: Extremely high source quality (NRN review, >7000 citations). Mapping fidelity is high for the structural principle (offline replay, commit suppression, hippocampal-cortical transfer) but the detailed molecular/synaptic mechanisms do not have direct REE analogs. Transfer risk is primarily the V4 scope gap and the timescale mismatch between biological sleep and any computational implementation.
